prompts.name: Live Runtime Proof Executor
prompts.useThisWhen: Static checks cannot prove the requested behavior.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
prompts.inspectFirst: Safety docs, launchers, sessions, runtime logs, proof chain.
prompts.expectedOutput: Observed behavior proof or honest bounded failure.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Observed behavior is not confused with ACK/static proof.
prompts.authority: Runtime mutation only inside safety boundary
prompts.text:
You are executing a live/runtime proof sprint.
Repo:
`xyz_repo_or_path`
Sprint:
`xyz_runtime_sprint`
Lane:
runtime proof / live behavior evidence
Scope:
prove the specific app/game/browser/service behavior under controlled conditions
Forbidden scope:
unrelated feature work, personal data mutation, save/account mutation, free resources, broad refactors, lower-proof success claims
Dependency:
Do not start until repo floor/hygiene is known safe and required launcher/focus/session safety is in place. If dependency is skipped, state why.
Before runtime:
- stop the app/game/service first if required by repo doctrine
- use repo-owned launcher/session scripts
- do not rely on terminal focus
- bound every wait
- use disposable/safe state only
- do not mutate personal saves/accounts/data
Required proof chain:
- stop/safe-start completed if required
- launcher/session attach succeeded
- target surface ready
- command or trigger issued
- command ACK observed if applicable
- behavior actually observed
- runtime artifact/log collected
- failure reason captured if behavior did not occur
Proof rule:
Do not claim live runtime proof from static tests, contract checks, launcher proof, command ACK, or route/request issuance alone. Name the exact proof level reached.
Validation:
- targeted build/test before runtime
- runtime proof collection
- log/artifact grep proving the required chain
- git diff --check
- git status --short
Final response:
runtime state used, completed work, live proof chain, validation output, generated artifacts/logs, changed files, known gaps/risks, git status, exact next command, PR URL if opened.

prompts.name: Read-Only Code Intelligence Catalog
prompts.useThisWhen: Agents repeatedly waste time finding seams, symbols, contracts, or entry points.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
prompts.inspectFirst: Codebase maps, MCP/LSP docs, source roots, workflow contracts.
prompts.expectedOutput: Read-only navigation tools, catalogs, and tests.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Navigation prerequisites and limits are honest.
prompts.proofCeiling: Navigation prerequisites and limits are honest.
prompts.text:
You are building a read-only code intelligence catalog for this repo.
Repo:
`xyz_repo_or_path`
Sprint:
Read-only MCP/LSP/code intelligence catalog
Lane:
local agent leverage
Scope:
deterministic read-only tools or docs that help future agents find policies, contracts, services, commands, handlers, and runtime seams without grep chaos
Forbidden scope:
runtime execution, target mutation, command inbox writes, ignored evidence reads by default, secret exposure, gameplay/product mutation
Read first:
- codebase map if present
- MCP/LSP docs if present
- workflow contracts
- harness scripts
- src/app code
- docs/handoff relevant to runtime or harness
Initial tool/catalog ideas:
- find_runtime_seam
- find_command_handler
- find_harness_contract
- find_policy_source
- find_workflow_spec
- find_artifact_schema
- outline_service
- outline_harness_script
Rules:
- read tracked docs/code/contracts by default
- exclude ignored logs, saves, runtime evidence, generated artifacts, and secrets
- return concise structured results
- symbol-backed tools must report missing_prereqs if LSP/project load is unavailable
- do not claim symbol navigation if only grep worked
Tests must prove:
- catalog exists
- allowed tools are read-only
- ignored evidence roots are excluded
- policy lookup finds docs
- result shape is JSON-serializable
- missing LSP reports planned/missing/missing_prereqs honestly
- no runtime execution strings appear in read-only catalog
Final response:
tool list, query examples, tests run, missing dependency behavior, gaps/risks, git state, exact next command, PR URL if opened.

prompts.name: Local Hook and Artifact Hygiene
prompts.sprintPathRole: Prevent secrets and generated junk from reaching commits
prompts.useThisWhen: Logs, evidence, secrets, or local artifacts threaten repo cleanliness.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
prompts.inspectFirst: Ignore rules, hooks, artifact paths, staged-file patterns.
prompts.expectedOutput: Guardrails, hooks, validators, and cleanup guidance.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Bad staged artifacts are blocked or warned.
prompts.proofCeiling: Bad staged artifacts are blocked or warned.
prompts.text:
You are adding local hook and artifact hygiene.
Repo:
`xyz_repo_or_path`
Sprint:
Local hook and artifact hygiene
Lane:
repo hygiene / safety rails
Scope:
prevent generated evidence, logs, saves, local tool installs, crash dumps, secrets, and machine-local junk from leaking into commits
Forbidden scope:
product behavior changes, runtime execution, mandatory global hooks, broad blockers that slow normal code/docs work
Read first:
- .gitignore
- .githooks if present
- hook installer if present
- harness docs
- artifact/output conventions
- existing tests around generated/runtime artifacts
Required behavior:
- hooks remain local opt-in unless repo already has mandatory policy
- generated/live/runtime evidence is blocked or warned clearly
- sanitized fixtures/docs remain commit-friendly
- hook output tells the operator what to do next
- no sensitive excerpts printed
- no broad blocking of normal docs/code changes
Good error wording:
[harness] refusing staged generated/runtime artifact: <path>
Move live/generated evidence back to ignored local output, or commit a sanitized fixture under an approved fixture/docs path.
Tests must prove:
- installer configures local hooks if installer exists
- pre-commit blocks obvious generated/runtime evidence
- remediation guidance appears
- sanitized fixtures are allowed
- docs/code are not broadly blocked
- hooks do not execute runtime/launcher/network activity
- hooks do not print sensitive excerpts
Final response:
hook behavior before/after, files changed, tests run, safety proof, false-positive notes, git state, exact next command, PR URL if opened.

prompts.name: End-to-End Harness Validator
prompts.useThisWhen: Implementation needs a repeatable PASS/SKIP/FAIL gate.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
prompts.inspectFirst: Tests, validators, schemas, fixtures, workflows, reports.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Skipped checks are not reported as passes.
prompts.text:
You are building an end-to-end synthetic harness validator.
Repo:
`xyz_repo_or_path`
Sprint:
One-command harness proof
Lane:
validation / harness proof
Scope:
offline/synthetic validator that aggregates safe harness checks and prints an honest PASS/SKIP/FAIL matrix
Forbidden scope:
live runtime execution, network probing, launcher execution, target mutation, save/account mutation, secret collection
Read first:
- existing validators
- run context / artifact registry if present
- report renderer if present
- workflow specs
- MCP/LSP readiness if present
- hook/artifact hygiene if present
Expected output:
APP HARNESS VALIDATION
[PASS] required files
[PASS] run context
[PASS] artifact registry
[PASS] report renderer
[SKIP] optional MCP symbol smoke: lsp_project_not_loaded
[PASS] hook hygiene
Result: 5 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed
Validator should:
1. detect repo root
2. print branch and commit
3. run safe offline validators
4. detect optional components
5. emit JSON result
6. emit English matrix
7. never claim runtime proof
8. fail clearly when a required validator is broken
Tests must prove:
- validator exists and parses
- prints matrix
- emits JSON
- does not launch app/game/browser unless explicitly runtime lane
- does not mutate targets/data
- skips optional missing dependencies honestly
Final response:
matrix behavior, dependencies detected, tests run, skipped checks and why, generated artifacts, git state, exact next command, PR URL if opened.

prompts.name: Final Handoff Compressor
prompts.useThisWhen: A serious sprint is complete or blocked and the next chat must continue cleanly.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
prompts.inspectFirst: Git state, PR state, changed files, artifacts, validation, gaps.
prompts.expectedOutput: Concise next-agent handoff and exact next command.
prompts.text:
COMPRESS THIS SPRINT INTO A NEXT-AGENT HANDOFF.
Include:
- repo
- branch
- PR/sprint
- lane
- owned scope
- forbidden scope
- what changed
- files changed
- artifacts produced
- validation run
- skipped checks
- known gaps
- risks
- important paths
- git state
- next command
- copy-paste prompt for the next agent
Also classify the proof level reached:
- contract proof
- harness proof
- static test proof
- build proof
- launcher/browser proof
- command ACK proof
- behavior observed proof
- live runtime proof
Do not include narrative fluff. Preserve only context that prevents rework. If parallel work is safe, name separate lanes and collision risks.
